# ANALYSIS: V14E_BEAR_ONLY_GATE_OP21_PROGRESS

**Filed:** 2026-05-31
**Filer:** chef-nemotron (free-tier autonomous R&D)
**Type:** analysis
**Status:** DRAFT (NEEDS-RATIFICATION per Rule 9)

## Hypothesis

We are analyzing the progress of the V14E_BEAR_ONLY_GATE candidate toward meeting the OP-21 promotion gate: WR >= 55% sustained over N>=100 new bear-only observations.

## Mechanism

The V14E_BEAR_ONLY_GATE watcher (exclude bull v14e branch) is live and accumulating bear-only observations. We need to count the number of new bear-only observations since the candidate was filed (2026-05-21) and compute the win rate.

## OP-20 disclosures

1. **Account-size assumption:** qty=28 requires $25K+; $1K paper ~= 14% headline (standard assumption for context; no live trading in observation accumulation)
2. **Sample bias:** Analyzing new bear-only observations since 2026-05-21. Sample size = count of new bear-only observations in watcher-observations.jsonl since filing date. Selection method: watcher logs observation when v14e bear-only condition met (score>=10, direction=bear). Overfit risk: low as no gate changes, pure observation.
3. **Out-of-sample:** NEEDS-OOS (live observation accumulation, not backtest)
4. **Real-fills:** NEEDS-REAL-FILLS (using watcher proxies, not real OPRA fills)
5. **Failure modes:** 
   - Worst day: consecutive losses could drop WR below 55%.
   - Max drawdown: not applicable (observation accumulation).
   - Blow-up: watcher failure to log observations halts accumulation.
6. **Concentration:** Will compute top-5 days % of P&L once 100+ observations reached; disclose X if available.

## Pre-merge gate

Accumulate 100+ new bear-only observations and verify WR >= 55%.

## Confidence

8 / 10 -- based on existing deduped bear WR=55.1% and raw bear WR=58.5% (pre-filing), confident new observations will meet gate if regime holds.

## Pre-existing leaderboard impact

This analysis complements the existing V14E_BEAR_ONLY_GATE candidate (rank 3) by tracking its OP-21 progress; no conflict with other candidates.
